name: The 'Lucky Break' Career Crash Recovery
description: Young professionals (early 20s) with non-technical degrees who landed 'lucky' high-paying tech roles are now permanently locked out of the industry after layoffs because they lack the formal credentials to pass modern ATS filters.
pain_points: Inability to pass resume screenings without a CS degree
Massive ego/emotional trauma from transitioning to manual labor
Ineligibility for entry-level roles due to being 'overqualified' but technically 'under-credentialed'
tech_stack: High-end CNC and Industrial Robotics hardware
